___MODDING_GUIDELINES___.1_FILE_PLACEMENT: Save this file in 'backend/world_data/worlds/'. The filename (e.g., 'wasteland.json') becomes your internal world_key.
___MODDING_GUIDELINES___.2_CG_VIDEOS: To use custom videos, place them in the 'mods/<world_key>/cgs/' folder. The engine ONLY supports .webm formats. Do not use MP4.
___MODDING_GUIDELINES___.3_CG_TRIGGERS: Use the 'cg_triggers' array to sniff keywords from the AI's output. If the AI generates 'Deathclaw', the engine will play the assigned video.
___MODDING_GUIDELINES___.4_ATTRIBUTES: You can rename the 7 core stats. Do not add new ones or remove existing ones. Map them in 'display_names'.
___MODDING_GUIDELINES___.5_BALANCING: Keep 'max_reward' around 5 and 'hp_max_penalty' around -15 to prevent the game from being too easy or impossible.

info.name: Post-Apocalyptic Wasteland
info.description: After the Great War, the surface became a scorched hellscape. Clean water is rarer than gold. Mutated creatures roam the ruins.
info.rules: Bottle caps are currency. Venturing outside requires a gas mask. Radiation constantly erodes the body.
info.special_notes: All events must fit a post-apocalyptic setting. Strictly NO magic, cultivation, or elves.

cg_triggers._comment: When the AI mentions 'Deathclaw', play these videos depending on if the player succeeds the dice roll.
Plays only during Boss/Node battles if the word 'Overseer' or 'Vault Door' appears.

descriptions.Strength: Melee damage, carry weight, physical tasks
descriptions.Perception: Ranged accuracy, trap detection, environmental awareness
descriptions.Caps: Wasteland currency for trading and hiring
descriptions.Luck: Critical hit chance, rare loot probability

character.birth_description: The vault's steel door slams shut behind you. Outside, the world is silent and dead...
character.backstory: Third-generation vault dweller. Raised underground, never seen the sky.

npcs.name: Doc Mercy
npcs.personality: Wasteland physician. Cynical but compassionate. Charges caps but treats the desperate for free.

relationships.family: Your parents were vault technicians who died in a reactor accident.

node_templates.16: The Overseer hands you a rusty pistol. 'The water purifier is dying. Find the replacement chip — or we all die down here.'
node_templates.20: Surrounded by a mutant warband. Firelight dances on their grotesque faces. Your weapon is low on ammo. Choose now, or die.
node_templates.30: A pre-war military bunker, supposedly containing technology that could change the wasteland forever. Inside could be salvation — or a trap.

world_specific_rules: [Wasteland Rules] Combat -> Strength/Perception. Trading -> Speech. High-rad zones -> Rad Resistance. [Penalty Style] Failure means radiation poisoning (-Rad Resistance) or injury (-HP).

age_constraints.0-15: [Vault Youth] Confined to the vault interior. Protected by adults. Cannot handle weapons.
age_constraints.16-30: [Wasteland Rookie] Just emerged onto the surface. Everything is a threat.
age_constraints.31-50: [Wasteland Veteran] Years of experience. Own territory, allies, and enemies.

0-15: Sneaking into the reactor room through a vent — the Geiger counter starts screaming.
First time holding a real gun. Your hands won't stop shaking.
16-30: A Deathclaw chases you through three city blocks. You dive into a wrecked school bus and pray.
You find a suit of pre-war power armor in a military depot. No fusion core, though.
31-50: Raiders hit your outpost at night. The sentry never got a chance to sound the alarm.

flavor_texts: Bottle caps jingle in your pocket...
